'Tariffs - use this version, please'
data.description:
A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Stella Qiu:

It is tariff deadline day and President Donald Trump delivered by slapping fresh levies on imports from dozens of countries, including those that do not have a trade deal yet.

Rates were set at 35% for Canada, 25% for India, 20% for Taiwan and 19% for Thailand. Switzerland got a whopping 39% — one of the steepest — raising the question: what's Trump got against the Swiss? Not buying enough American chocolate or watches?

The big day comes after months of posturing, meetings, delays and truces, which prompted some investors to question what was a real threat and what was a bluff. Indeed, there is still much to be resolved.

Arguably, most levies are lower than those threatened on April 2, which back then sent markets into a tailspin. Plus the big trade deals with Japan and the European Union have been reached while talks with China and Mexico are still ongoing.

That is probably why market reaction this time has been much more muted. Sure, most Asian shares fell, but only modestly. South Korea is an exception, tumbling over 3%, in part due to domestic tax cuts being rolled back.

Taiwan's president said the 20% levy is only temporary and is expected to be reduced further when a deal is reached.

Wall Street and European shares did not seem to be too bothered by the tariff news. EUROSTOXX 50 futures slipped 0.3%. Both Nasdaq futures and S&P 500 futures fell 0.2%, thanks to a 6% tumble in Amazon after its earnings failed to meet lofty expectations.

Now, with the tariff news out of the way, euro zone flash CPI is due later in the day and expectations are for a slight easing to 1.9% in July from 2.0% in annual terms. Markets have only priced in half a cut from the European Central Bank by early next year.

And then it's all about waiting for payrolls, which will be pivotal for hopes for a rate cut from the Federal Reserve in September, which is now priced at just 40%, way off 75% a month ago.

Forecasts are centred on a 110,000 rise in July, while the jobless rate likely ticked up to 4.2% from 4.1%. Any upside surprises could price out the chance of a move next month, giving dollar bulls another reason to rally.

The greenback is headed for the best week - with a gain of 2.5% against its peers - in nearly three years, solidifying its recent uptrend from a three-year low.

It has found support from a hawkish Fed that has held off policy easing on tariff risks. And indeed, the Fed's preferred gauge of inflation came in a tad hotter overnight, showing some tariff impact.

Key developments that could influence markets on Friday:

-- Euro zone flash CPI for July

-- U.S. payrolls for July, ISM Manufacturing survey

Trying to keep up with the latest tariff news?

Our new daily news digest offers a rundown of the top market-moving headlines impacting global trade. Sign up for Tariff Watch here.

data.title: Pietu Parikka has started as Wetteri's new CEO on 1 August, 2025
data.description:
Wetteri Oyj

Wetteri Plc

Media release

1 August, 2025 at 7.30 a.m. EET

Pietu Parikka has started as Wetteri's new CEO on 1 August, 2025

Wetteri announced on 1 July, 2025, a change in its CEO position. Wetteri's new CEO, Pietu Parikka starts today, 1 August 2025. The company's long-serving CEO, Aarne Simula, will continue to act as a member of the Board of Directors and as an advisor for the management, and he will also retain his position as the largest shareholder in Wetteri.

Pietu Parikka has acted as the CFO and Chief Operating Officer at Wetteri since April 2025. With his previous positions in Hedin Automotive Oy and Laakkonen Autokonserni as CEO and CFO, he has several years of experience in automotive industry. Parikka holds a M.Sc. degree in Economics.  

"I thank the Board of Directors of Wetteri for this great opportunity, and for their trust in me. I see tremendous potential in the company to grow into a profitable and higher-quality car trader. I will do my best to ensure Wetteri's success, together with the company's personnel," Pietu Parikka states.

In May, Wetteri announced the launch of a strategy update process, with a focus on improving profitability and operational efficiency. The company will publish its new strategy in autumn 2025.

Parikka's photo and résumé are available at the company website: https://sijoittajat.wetteri.fi/en/governance/ceo-and-management-team/.

Further information and interview requests:

Wetteri's communications

Tel. +358 50 3369 785, viestinta@wetteri.fi

Wetteri Plc - an entrepreneur-driven growth company in the automotive industry

Wetteri Plc is an entrepreneur-driven growth company in the automotive industry. In addition to the retail trade of passenger, commercial and heavy-duty vehicles, the company provides maintenance and damage repair services ranging from passenger cars to heavy-duty vehicles. The company has 35 offices in Finland, and its head office is located in Oulu. The company employs approximately 800 people, of whom approximately 76% work in maintenance and damage repair services. Wetteri is a promoter of the digitalisation of the automotive industry and an important player in the joint journey towards emission-free driving. More information: www.sijoittajat.wetteri.fi/en/.

https://news.cision.com/wetteri-oyj/r/pietu-parikka-has-started-as-wetteri-s-new-ceo-on-1-august--2025%2Cc4176589

data.title: India's Restaurant Brands Asia rises after Q1 loss narrows
data.description:
** Shares of Restaurant Brands Asia rise as much as 4.2% to 84.8 rupees

** Indian Burger King operator reported a narrower Q1 loss of 419.4 mln rupees ($4.8 mln) as its discounted menu items continued to draw in budget-conscious diners

** Centrum Broking believes RESR showcased strong performance despite subdued consumer spends and rising competition, and that further cost-reduction initiatives along with recovery in dine-in help to boost the business

** Stock on track to snap 2 days of losses

** Analysts' mean rating on stock is "buy"; median PT is 92.5 rupees - data compiled by LSEG

** RESR last up 3.8%, cutting YTD losses to 4.1%

($1 = 87.3550 Indian rupees)

data.title: Bitcoin dips below $115K as Trump tariff order fails to comfort investors
data.description:
Bitcoin has fallen to its lowest level in three weeks as US President Donald Trump released an executive order imposing trade tariffs on a raft of countries. 

Bitcoin fell to $114,250 on Coinbase during early trading in Asia on Friday, according to TradingView. It hasn’t been that low since June 11.

The original cryptocurrency has now broken below its three-week range-bound channel, and the next support zone lies at around $111,000 if there is no rebound. Today’s 2.6% decline takes the asset around 6.5% below its all-time high of $122,800, which came on July 14. 

Bitcoin had already been falling in the lead-up to the tariff decision deadline, with $110 billion having exited spot crypto markets over the past 12 hours.

A total of 158,000 traders were liquidated to the tune of $630 million over the past 24 hours, according to CoinGlass. Most of these were long positions.

The crypto market dip comes in the same week that the White House issued a crypto policy report widely considered bullish for the industry.

Trump imposes trade tariffs 

President Trump late on Thursday formalized an array of high tariffs and trade deals he’s announced in recent weeks, including hiking tariffs on Canada from 25% to 35%. 

The executive order, which came before a self-imposed deadline for trade deals, also set rates for countries that did not reach agreements with him, such as South Africa, Switzerland, Taiwan and Thailand, which are facing tariffs between 19% and 39%. 

Agreements with prominent trading partners such as the European Union, Japan, South Korea and the United Kingdom were also made official. 

Stock markets across Asia traded lower on Friday morning, along with crypto markets. 

China deal could ease tensions

“Combined with the uncertainty over tariffs, it is natural to see some profit taking after a very strong run both in equities and crypto markets,” Apollo Capital’s chief investment officer, Henrik Andersson, told Cointelegraph, adding: 

“If a deal with China can be made, it would remove a lot of the current uncertainty in our opinion.”

“This week’s market dip reflects a mix of tariff deadline fear and broader macroeconomic uncertainty, with Trump’s new plans amplifying volatility,” Nick Ruck, director at LVRG Research, told Cointelegraph.

However, some investors viewed the sell-off as a “temporary correction rather than a structural shift,” he said before adding: 

“While tariffs contributed to the pullback, the dump was likely exacerbated by profit-taking after recent ATHs, lingering geopolitical tensions, and US macro uncertainty.”

Highest monthly candle closes

Despite the relatively minor Bitcoin dip, the asset posted its highest-ever monthly candle in July. It hit an all-time high and closed the month at $115,784, according to TradingView.

It was not the largest monthly candle, however. That was last November, when the asset surged by $26,000 in just one month following the election of Donald Trump in the US.

data.title: Thai Central Bank Likely to Cut Rates Two More Times This Year — Market Talk
data.description: The Bank of Thailand is likely to cut rates two more times this year, given muted price pressures and a weak growth outlook, ANZ Research economists write in a report. Thailand's consumer prices likely continued to decline further in July, they say. This reflects lower raw food and energy prices as well as a drag from base effects, they say. Thailand's consumer price index declined 0.25% from a year earlier in June, while core CPI rose 1.06%. ANZ expects Thailand's headline inflation to have declined 0.5% and core inflation to have risen 0.93% in July. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Malaysia Views U.S. Tariff Cut as Positive Outcome
data.description:
By Ying Xian Wong

KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia--The U.S. decision to reduce tariffs on Malaysian exports to 19% from 25% is a positive outcome, and the government will work closely with relevant parties to mitigate the impact on exports, Malaysia's trade ministry said.

The outcome follows nearly three months of sustained engagement between the two countries, and the 19% rate brings Malaysia roughly in line with other Southeast Asian economies, the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry said Friday.

Malaysia stood firm on various "red line" items and achieved the rate without compromising on its sovereign right to implement key policies, MITI said, and views the lower rate as "a significant achievement of Malaysia's thorough and methodical negotiating process".

MITI will work with other ministries and agencies to manage the tariff impact on exporters, and push industrial reform under the country's development blueprints.

Despite global economic headwinds and rising geopolitical tensions, MITI said Malaysia's domestic demand remains robust and private consumption continues to support growth.

MITI will roll out outreach programs in the coming months to help exporters adjust to the revised tariff structure.

The U.S. is Malaysia's largest export destination, with shipments valued at 198.65 billion ringgit, equivalent to $46.58 billion, in 2024. It is also the top source of foreign investment, with 32.82 ringgit billion in approved investments last year.

Write to Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com

data.title: South Korean Shares Fall Further; Tax Increase Plan Weighs — Market Talk
data.description: South Korea's benchmark Kospi falls more than 3%, extending early losses. The new left-leaning Seoul government's tax-increase plan is weighing on sentiment, says NH Investment & Securities market analyst Kim Jong-young. The plan announced overnight proposes raising the corporate tax by one percentage point to the maximum 25% and the stock transaction tax to 0.2% from 0.15%. Semiconductor, defense and steel stocks are among the top decliners. Foreign and institutional investors remain net sellers. Memory-chip maker SK Hynix drops 5.5%, steelmaker Posco falls 4.7% and defense company Hanwha Aerospace slides 6.5%. The index is last down 3.3% at 3138.62. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

data.title: Zydus Lifesciences shares fall 2.01% in early trade
data.description:
Shares of Zydus Lifesciences declined by 2.01% to Rs 950.35 in early trade. The stock is a part of the Nifty Next 50 index. This decline reflects a notable price movement in today's session.

The financial performance of Zydus Lifesciences shows a consolidated revenue of Rs 6,527.90 Crore for the quarter-ending March 2025, compared to Rs 5,533.80 Crore for the quarter-ending March 2024. The net profit for the same period stood at Rs 1,248.80 Crore, up from Rs 1,229.00 Crore year-over-year. The EPS was reported at Rs 11.64 for March 2025 against Rs 11.69 in March 2024.

The company's annual financial performance also indicates strong growth. Consolidated revenue for the year-ending March 2025 reached Rs 23,241.50 Crore, significantly higher than the Rs 19,547.40 Crore recorded for the year-ending March 2024. The net profit for the year-ending March 2025 was Rs 4,614.80 Crore, compared to Rs 3,831.40 Crore for the year-ending March 2024. data.title: HUL shares up 3.88%, among top gainers on Nifty 50 in early trade
data.description:
Shares of HUL were trading higher in Friday's session. The stock is among the top gainers on Nifty 50 in early trade. Currently priced at Rs 2,619, the stock has gained 3.88% at 9:30 am from its previous close.

Hindustan Unilever Limited is featured in the benchmark Nifty 50 index.

Recent Financial Performance:

HUL has demonstrated consistent financial performance over the past few years. The consolidated revenue for the year ending March 2025 stood at Rs 63,121 Crore, marking an increase from Rs 61,896 Crore in the previous year. The net profit for the same period was Rs 10,679 Crore, up from Rs 10,286 Crore in March 2024. The company's EPS (Earnings Per Share) also saw an increase, rising to Rs 45.32 in March 2025 from Rs 43.74 in March 2024.

The company’s quarterly performance shows a similar trend. The revenue for the quarter-ending June 2025 was Rs 16,514 Crore, up from Rs 15,707 Crore in June 2024. The net profit for the quarter-ending June 2025 was Rs 2,769 Crore, compared to Rs 2,614 Crore in June 2024. The EPS for the quarter-ending June 2025 was Rs 11.73, up from Rs 11.11 in June 2024.

data.title: Hindustan Unilever jumps on gradual earnings recovery hopes
data.description:
India's Hindustan Unilever shares rose 4% on Friday, extending its gains from Thursday, after it reported its first-quarter profit climbed, with growth in revenue and sales volume pointing to a gradual earnings recovery.

The consumer goods maker's stock was the top percentage gainer on the benchmark Nifty 50 index , down 0.2% in early trade.

It was also among the top percentage gainers on the fast moving consumer goods sub-index , which ticked 1.4% higher.

It closed 3.44% higher on Thursday after it announced its results.

HUL's first-quarter volume growth was slightly better than estimated, noted brokerage Macquarie.

"We like HUL's volume-led growth strategy among growing competitive intensity..." said brokerage HSBC.

data.title: Ringgit May Trade Around 4.25 Versus Dollar Next Week — Market Talk
data.description: The ringgit may trade around 4.25 against the dollar next week, helped by easing tariff concerns, Kenanga economists say in a note. Malaysia's reduced tariff rate of 19% is seen as a positive for equities and could attract modest foreign inflows, lending near-term support to the currency, they say. Markets are watching for the U.S. labor report due later, which could influence expectations of future Fed rate cuts. A softer jobs print may weaken the dollar and lift risk appetite, they say. USD/MYR faces resistance at 4.30 and support at 4.25 in the short term, they add. USD/MYR is 0.3% higher at 4.2785.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Dollar Recovers as Tariff Uncertainty Eases — WSJ
data.description:
By Peter Landers

The U.S. dollar, which took a dive against global currencies after President Trump's "liberation day" tariff announcement in April, is recovering some ground this week. As investors get a clearer picture of Trump's tariffs-including a common 15% rate for major trading partners such as the European Union, Japan and South Korea-doubts about the dollar's longstanding status as the main global reserve currency have ebbed for now. The Federal Reserve's decision this week to keep rates steady also lifted the dollar.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Capstone Copper Corp reports results for the quarter ended June 30 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Capstone Copper Corp reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 4 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended June 30, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 3 cents. The mean expectation of fifteen analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 5 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 2 cents to 12 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 38.2% to $543.20 million from a year ago; analysts expected $561.05 million.

  • Capstone Copper Corp's reported EPS for the quarter was 3 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $24 million.

  • Capstone Copper Corp shares had fallen by 9.9% this quarter and lost 15.3% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 0.3% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, five analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 16 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the specialty mining & metals peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Capstone Copper Corp is C$12.00, about 35.3% above its last closing price of C$7.77

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data August 1 at 04:11 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Asia Stock Markets Fall as Trump Sets New Tariffs for Dozens of Countries
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao

Asia-Pacific equity markets fell broadly, following U.S. stock futures lower, after President Trump announced steeper tariffs against dozens of countries while delaying the effective date by a week.

The U.S. will raise tariffs on scores of nations, effective at 12:01 a.m. on Aug. 7, according to an executive order Trump signed on Thursday. When the action is taken, the result will be significantly higher duties on virtually every U.S. trading partner.

Trump has set a 15% tariff with major industrialized economies including the European Union, Japan and South Korea.

Other nations with which the U.S. has a small trade deficit would also get 15% tariffs, a senior administration official told reporters. Countries with which the U.S. runs a trade surplus would be hit with 10% tariffs, the official said

Taiwan's Taiex fell 0.7% on Friday, while Japan's Nikkei Stock Average was 0.4% lower. South Korea's Kospi dropped 3.3% and Australia's S&P/ASX 200 shed 0.8%.

Futures tied to U.S. indexes edged lower. Nasdaq and S&P 500 futures were down about 0.2% each, while Dow futures lost 0.1%.

The Trump administration's decision gives nations another week to finalize trade deals that could lower their levies. The U.S. has secured agreements with a number of countries in recent weeks, including major trading partners.

Tariff-related developments will likely "remain fluid in the coming weeks," DBS economists Radhika Rao and Chua Han Teng said in a note.

The tariffs were expected for Asia, with larger export countries reasonably facing lower duties than Southeast Asian nations, said Lorraine Tan, director of equity research for Asia at Morningstar. Markets will likely shrug off this news, she said in a note. "The main focus is China, where negotiations are ongoing."

Trading was choppy in Hong Kong, with the Hang Seng Index wavering between slight gains and losses. The benchmark index was recently down 0.2%. China's Shanghai Composite Index was also 0.2% lower.

Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com

data.title: SNG: Saudi, Russian energy ministers discuss oil market and joint committee plans
data.description:
First published: 01-Aug-2025 08:14:20

Staff Writer

RIYADH — Saudi Energy Minister Prince Abdulaziz bin Salman and Russian Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak held a meeting in Riyadh to discuss recent developments in the global oil market and ongoing cooperation between the two countries within the OPEC+ alliance.

As co-chairs of the Saudi-Russian Joint Governmental Committee on Trade, Economic, Scientific, and Technical Cooperation, the two officials also reviewed progress since the committee’s eighth session, including the launch of direct flights between Saudi Arabia and Russia and the signing of multiple memorandums of understanding in fields such as industry, education, media, and Hajj and Umrah.

The meeting also covered ways to boost bilateral trade and expand economic collaboration.

Both sides discussed the upcoming ninth session of the joint committee, scheduled to be held in Riyadh on November 6, 2025.

According to a statement from the Saudi Ministry of Energy, the ministers reaffirmed the importance of coordination on oil production policy under OPEC+.

Russian news agency Interfax reported that Novak and Prince Abdulaziz discussed oil market trends and the outlook for deeper cooperation between the two energy producers.

Their meeting followed Monday’s virtual session of the OPEC+ Joint Ministerial Monitoring Committee (JMMC), which called for full compliance with output agreements. 

A separate meeting among eight OPEC+ members is expected this Sunday to decide on a potential oil production increase for September.

Goldman Sachs has forecasted that OPEC+ will announce a final production hike of around 550,000 barrels per day for September.
